jokes.text: My campaign has been hijacked.
jokes.setup: Moira dramatically enters declaring hijacking
jokes.punchline: It's just missing lawn signs
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira overdramatization - treats missing lawn signs as a major political conspiracy. Strong character integration.

jokes.text: Maybe people were mowing their lawns.
jokes.setup: Moira's dramatic conspiracy theories about missing signs
jokes.punchline: Mundane explanation - lawn mowing
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Moira's drama with practical explanation. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: Oh, like the wind.
jokes.setup: Someone suggests 'sinister explanation' for missing signs
jokes.punchline: Wind as the sinister force
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one line that deflates conspiracy thinking. Strong deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: Oh, this woman may not have many signs, but she has myriad qualities worth voting for. I must stop her!
jokes.setup: Moira imagining enemy's thought process
jokes.punchline: Elaborate internal monologue of sign stealer
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira self-aggrandizement - imagines elaborate conspiracy against her minimal campaign. Strong character voice.

jokes.text: Stolen? Ew!
jokes.setup: Discussion of sign theft
jokes.punchline: Moira's disgusted reaction to the word 'stolen'
jokes.explanation: Moira's prim reaction to crude political reality. Good character moment with perfect economy.

jokes.text: Someone is imitated enough into thinking that I might be affecting this town's long-established voting patterns.
jokes.setup: Moira finds silver lining in sign theft
jokes.punchline: Mangled word 'imitated' instead of 'intimidated'
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira malapropism combined with her ego. Characteristic verbal fumbling while maintaining grandiosity.

jokes.text: Yeah, I was worried Jocelyn didn't have enough signs up.
jokes.setup: Roland mentions playing field being more fair now
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comment about Jocelyn's excessive signage
jokes.explanation: Good sarcasm acknowledging the obvious sign disparity. Sets up the visual comedy of excessive campaigning.

jokes.text: Well, I would like my little lady to win this campaign.
jokes.setup: Discussion of campaign signs
jokes.punchline: Roland's condescending 'little lady' reference
jokes.explanation: Roland's casual sexism creates cringe humor. Good character consistency but predictable.

jokes.text: Oh. Moira had lawn signs?
jokes.setup: Johnny mentions Moira's signs disappeared
jokes.punchline: Roland's surprise that she had any signs at all
jokes.explanation: Perfect beat showing how minimal Moira's campaign was. Good economy and timing.

jokes.text: Well, if I find out that you're accusing me of doing something I didn't do, then I'm going to accuse you of making false accusations.
jokes.setup: Johnny's veiled accusation about sign theft
jokes.punchline: Roland's circular logic threat
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic from Roland. Structure gets convoluted but the circular reasoning is funny.

jokes.text: Fine. But if you're denying doing something you might have done and making me feel guilty... For accusing you of something you claim you didn't do...
jokes.setup: Continuing circular argument with Roland
jokes.punchline: Johnny getting lost in his own convoluted logic
jokes.explanation: Escalation of the circular logic but becomes unwieldy. Shows Johnny getting pulled into Roland's nonsense.

jokes.text: Maybe.
jokes.setup: After saying he won't tell Roland something
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermining his own threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect comic deflation with single word. Excellent economy and timing - Johnny can't help himself.

jokes.text: You were my mistake, which I take full credit for.
jokes.setup: Wendy firing David apologetically
jokes.punchline: Taking credit for her 'mistake' while firing him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant paradox - taking pride in admitting a mistake while delivering bad news. Perfect Wendy character moment.

jokes.text: But that's not gonna help me when I am down to a Costco card and a zoo membership.
jokes.setup: David mentions they sold a poncho
jokes.punchline: Wendy's dire financial straits described through random memberships
jokes.explanation: Specific and absurd detail about financial ruin. The random zoo membership makes it memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: That there's another store called Blouse Barn? No.
jokes.setup: Wendy asks if David can believe there's another Blouse Barn
jokes.punchline: David's deadpan response about the terrible name
jokes.explanation: Perfect David reaction - deadpan dismissal of the name quality. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Apparently, I have been using the name without their permission.
jokes.setup: Describing payment as a 'gesture'
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's actually a legal settlement
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - what seemed like good news is actually Wendy getting in trouble. Well-structured reveal.

jokes.text: And to think I almost called this place 'Blouse House.'
jokes.setup: Discussion of name trademark issues
jokes.punchline: Wendy's relief at avoiding an even worse name
jokes.explanation: Wendy's obliviousness to how bad both names are. Good character consistency and wordplay.

jokes.text: Lotta drunks.
jokes.setup: David mentions having trouble with Australians
jokes.punchline: Casual racist generalization delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Classic David casual prejudice delivered matter-of-factly. Economy is perfect but relies on stereotype.

jokes.text: If there's one thing I've learned from 'The Good Wife', it's never accept a first offer.
jokes.setup: David offering business advice
jokes.punchline: Getting wisdom from TV show
jokes.explanation: Perfect David moment - treating TV drama as business school. Good character integration and quotability.

jokes.text: Yes, your cologne is too strong.
jokes.setup: David says he has a question
jokes.punchline: Johnny answers a different question entirely
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection and perfect economy. Shows Johnny's practical directness vs David's vanity.

jokes.text: That was a fun shakedown.
jokes.setup: Johnny describing going after trademark violators
jokes.punchline: Describing legal action as 'fun shakedown'
jokes.explanation: Johnny's casual attitude toward aggressive business tactics. Good character reveal of his ruthless side.

jokes.text: Well, first of all, I'd never get into the clothing business 'cause the margins are terrible...
jokes.setup: David asks Johnny to hypothetically own Blouse Barn
jokes.punchline: Johnny immediately critiques the business model
jokes.explanation: Perfect Johnny - can't engage with hypothetical without business analysis first. Strong character integration.

jokes.text: I would take that in a second. It's a terrible name.
jokes.setup: Asked about $10,000 for Blouse Barn name
jokes.punchline: Immediate acceptance because name is awful
jokes.explanation: Johnny's blunt business assessment. Good economy and character voice - no sentimentality about bad branding.

jokes.text: Technically, I would say I'm in a transition phase at the moment.
jokes.setup: Johnny asking if David was fired
jokes.punchline: David's euphemistic description of being fired
jokes.explanation: Classic David deflection - can't admit to failure directly. Good character consistency with his vanity.

jokes.text: Well, thank you so much for this. Um, it was entirely unhelpful, but thank you nevertheless.
jokes.setup: David getting advice from Johnny
jokes.punchline: Politely acknowledging the advice was useless
jokes.explanation: Perfect passive-aggressive politeness. David's way of being insulting while maintaining social niceties.

jokes.text: That's kind of like my aunt. She has a ghost in her house that keeps leaving dimes everywhere.
jokes.setup: Discussion of break-in that left dead bird
jokes.punchline: Twyla's random ghost story comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Twyla non-sequitur - completely random but delivered with conviction. Highly quotable and original.

jokes.text: Oh no, I'd put one on my lawn if I wasn't worried about winning.
jokes.setup: Offering to help with Moira's campaign signs
jokes.punchline: Admitting she won't help because she wants to win
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jocelyn honesty - offers help then immediately explains why she won't give it. Good character moment.

jokes.text: Were you picked up by the South Korean Secret Police on New Year's?
jokes.setup: Alexis claiming she's better under pressure than David
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning international incident
jokes.explanation: Classic Alexis backstory - casually mentions outrageous international incident. Excellent character integration and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Did you or did you not have a subscription to Cosmo Girl when you were 16 years old, David?
jokes.setup: Alexis preparing David for tough negotiation
jokes.punchline: Embarrassing personal detail as interrogation practice
jokes.explanation: Alexis using sibling knowledge as negotiation training. Good character dynamics and specific embarrassing detail.

jokes.text: No. Okay, David, if you have to lie about something you should breathe out while you answer 'cause it really just calms you down.
jokes.setup: David denying the Cosmo Girl subscription
jokes.punchline: Alexis giving lying advice based on his obvious tell
jokes.explanation: Alexis's practical approach to deception, delivered as helpful advice. Shows her experience with high-stakes situations.

jokes.text: I'm not gonna wear my own clothes into the room, David. They're not scary enough.
jokes.setup: Alexis raiding Moira's closet for meeting
jokes.punchline: Her own clothes aren't intimidating enough
jokes.explanation: Good Alexis logic - needing scarier clothes for business. Shows her understanding of fashion as armor.

jokes.text: If Prince Harry trusted me with his life, then I think you can too.
jokes.setup: Convincing Wendy to trust her in negotiation
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to life-or-death situation with royalty
jokes.explanation: Another classic Alexis backstory bomb. Perfect character voice and highly quotable royal reference.

jokes.text: Oh my God, you're so cute. Um, I'm not a lawyer, but I'm glad that my fashion pony is working.
jokes.setup: Wendy assumes Alexis is a lawyer
jokes.punchline: Alexis admits she's just dressed the part
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection reveal and 'fashion pony' is perfect Alexis terminology. Shows her confidence in appearance over credentials.

jokes.text: Angelica Bloomfield of Rose, Bloomfield and Glickman.
jokes.setup: Introducing herself to lawyers
jokes.punchline: Made-up impressive law firm name
jokes.explanation: Alexis improvising fancy lawyer identity. Using 'Rose' from family name adds nice touch to the deception.

jokes.text: She accidentally, uh, threw them out.
jokes.setup: Stevie found Moira's signs in their garbage
jokes.punchline: Johnny's weak excuse for obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment as Johnny tries to cover for Moira's scheme with obvious lie. Stevie's silence makes it worse.

jokes.text: Or... someone stole them and hid them in our garbage to try and sabotage Moira... to make it look like she's done something... really crazy.
jokes.setup: Trying to explain signs in garbage
jokes.punchline: Elaborate conspiracy theory that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Johnny digging deeper into absurd explanations. Gets convoluted but shows his panic about covering for Moira.

jokes.text: Objection sustained.
jokes.setup: David points out redundant legal arguments
jokes.punchline: Alexis using wrong legal term confidently
jokes.explanation: Alexis using legal terminology incorrectly but with confidence. Good economy and shows her faking expertise.

jokes.text: Which is very similar to the meals and not necessarily relevant, so...
jokes.setup: Alexis listing 'catering' as separate expense from meals
jokes.punchline: David's practical correction of redundant items
jokes.explanation: David's practical nature cutting through Alexis's nonsense. Good sibling dynamic and realistic correction.

jokes.text: Politics 101, John. When you have limited resources, your best course of action is to create a stir.
jokes.setup: Johnny discovering Moira took her own signs
jokes.punchline: Moira treating self-sabotage as sophisticated strategy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira - turning obvious failure into sophisticated political strategy. Excellent character integration and quotable.

jokes.text: It's like that episode of Sunrise Bay when I stole my own baby.
jokes.setup: Explaining her political strategy
jokes.punchline: Comparing to soap opera plot where she stole her own baby
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to Moira's acting past with absurd soap plot. Perfect character integration and incredibly quotable.

jokes.text: Moira, Sunrise Bay was a soap opera. Yes, and this is reality.
jokes.setup: Johnny pointing out soap opera comparison is ridiculous
jokes.punchline: Moira doubling down that reality should work like soap opera
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Moira's delusion - she thinks reality should work like soap opera drama. Excellent character moment.

jokes.text: You shouldn't make accusations you can't substantiate.
jokes.setup: Johnny accused Roland of stealing signs that Moira actually took
jokes.punchline: Moira lecturing Johnny while being the actual culprit
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - Moira giving moral lecture while being completely guilty. Strong character moment.

jokes.text: Well, the point is, judging by your bag and those shoes, Blouse Barn Australia is paying you quite a bit of money
jokes.setup: Negotiating with Australian lawyers
jokes.punchline: Using fashion knowledge to assess their wealth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis - using fashion expertise as business intelligence. Excellent character integration of her skills.

jokes.text: And let's be honest, 'Blouse House' doesn't have quite the same ring to it.
jokes.setup: Discussing alternative business names
jokes.punchline: Referencing Wendy's earlier terrible name option
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing David was paying attention and using the information strategically.

jokes.text: And let's be honest, 'Blouse House' doesn't have quite the same ring to it.
jokes.setup: Negotiating name rights
jokes.punchline: Referencing Wendy's terrible alternative name
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier terrible name suggestion. Shows Alexis using all available information in negotiation.

jokes.text: We're in our store.
jokes.setup: Alexis saying they have another meeting to get to
jokes.punchline: David pointing out they're already in their store
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan reality check. Excellent economy and timing - deflates Alexis's dramatic exit strategy.

jokes.text: Roland got carried away and used his mayoral power to get people to put more signs on their lawns.
jokes.setup: Explaining Roland's excessive signage
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of political corruption
jokes.explanation: Jocelyn's casual admission of corruption shows small-town politics. Good character consistency with her honesty.

jokes.text: The other day, I called another driver an effing moron. I didn't say the 'f' word, but I said, 'effing,'
jokes.setup: Jocelyn explaining how campaign stress is affecting her
jokes.punchline: Her idea of extreme profanity is saying 'effing'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jocelyn - her version of extreme behavior is incredibly mild. Excellent character integration and specific detail.

jokes.text: I can't get through an episode of 'Masterchef Junior' without crying.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn explaining stress symptoms
jokes.punchline: Specific, innocent show causing emotional breakdown
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific detail showing Jocelyn's emotional state. The innocent choice of show makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I can't get through an episode of 'Masterchef Junior' without crying.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn listing campaign stress symptoms
jokes.punchline: Crying at children's cooking show
jokes.explanation: Specific and absurd detail about Jocelyn's emotional state. Perfect character moment showing her sensitivity.

jokes.text: Congratulations, Moira. You are the new councilor for the town of Schitt's Creek.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn dropping out of race
jokes.punchline: Moira 'winning' by default after all her scheming
jokes.explanation: Ironic victory - Moira gets what she wanted through opponent's breakdown rather than her scheming. Good plot payoff.

jokes.text: Um, you don't mean one of those big lottery checks, right?
jokes.setup: Wendy mentions a 'big check'
jokes.punchline: David's specific clarification about novelty checks
jokes.explanation: David's practical concern about literal interpretation. Good character moment showing his attention to detail.

jokes.text: Four years? No, no one said anything about four years.
jokes.setup: Johnny mentions what she can do in four years on council
jokes.punchline: Moira's horror at actual term length
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira moment - winning without understanding the commitment. Shows her impulsiveness and lack of planning.

jokes.text: Shouldn't we be s-s-s... Should we be... Trying to sa... save this?
jokes.setup: Family discussing spending windfall money
jokes.punchline: David struggling to say 'save' like it's foreign concept
jokes.explanation: Brilliant physical comedy - David literally can't say 'save' because it's so foreign to the family. Perfect character integration showing their spending habits.

jokes.text: Just 'cause I do my own nails doesn't mean, you know, I wouldn't go...
jokes.setup: Family planning manicures celebration
jokes.punchline: Johnny's defensive admission about doing his own nails
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Johnny's hidden vanity and defensiveness about his grooming habits.
